versions.title: The Agent Loop
versions.subtitle: One Loop Is All You Need
versions.keyInsight: The smallest useful agent is a loop that calls the model, runs tools, and feeds results back.

versions.title: Tool Use
versions.subtitle: Add a Tool, Add Just One Line
versions.keyInsight: The loop stays stable while capabilities register into a dispatch table.

versions.title: Permission
versions.subtitle: Check Permissions Before Execution
versions.keyInsight: Dangerous actions need a harness decision point before the shell runs.

versions.title: Hooks
versions.subtitle: Hang on the Loop, Don't Write into It
versions.keyInsight: Cross-cutting behavior belongs around the loop, not tangled inside it.

versions.title: TodoWrite
versions.subtitle: An Agent Without a Plan Drifts Off Course
versions.keyInsight: Explicit plans keep long-running work visible and correctable.

versions.title: Subagent
versions.subtitle: Break Large Tasks into Small Ones with Clean Context
versions.keyInsight: Subagents give each subtask a clean message history while preserving the main thread.

versions.title: Skill Loading
versions.subtitle: Load Only When Needed
versions.keyInsight: Inject specialized knowledge only when the task actually needs it.

versions.title: Context Compact
versions.subtitle: Context Will Fill Up
versions.keyInsight: Compression keeps the conversation usable when the context window gets crowded.

versions.title: Memory
versions.subtitle: Keep a Layer That Doesn't Lose Details
versions.keyInsight: Some facts should survive summarization and future sessions.

versions.title: System Prompt
versions.subtitle: Assembled at Runtime, Never Hardcoded
versions.keyInsight: The system prompt is a generated product of policy, tools, skills, and context.

versions.title: Error Recovery
versions.subtitle: Errors Are the Start of a Retry
versions.keyInsight: A robust harness classifies failures and decides what kind of retry is worthwhile.

versions.title: Task System
versions.subtitle: Break Big Goals into Small Tasks
versions.keyInsight: A task graph turns vague goals into ordered, observable work.

versions.title: Background Tasks
versions.subtitle: Slow Operations Go to the Background
versions.keyInsight: The agent can keep reasoning while slow work completes elsewhere.

versions.title: Cron Scheduler
versions.subtitle: Producing Work on a Schedule
versions.keyInsight: Recurring work should be created by the harness, not remembered by the model.

versions.title: Agent Teams
versions.subtitle: One Agent Isn't Enough, Form a Team
versions.keyInsight: Persistent teammates let work continue in parallel without stuffing every thought into one context.

versions.title: Team Protocols
versions.subtitle: Teammates Need Agreements
versions.keyInsight: Multi-agent systems need explicit message contracts, not vibes.

versions.title: Autonomous Agents
versions.subtitle: Check the Board, Claim the Task
versions.keyInsight: Teammates become useful when they can discover and claim work themselves.

versions.title: Worktree Isolation
versions.subtitle: Separate Directories, No Conflicts
versions.keyInsight: Parallel agents need isolated filesystems as much as isolated conversations.

versions.title: MCP Tools
versions.subtitle: External Tools, Standard Protocol
versions.keyInsight: External services can become agent tools through a standard discovery and call protocol.

versions.title: Comprehensive Agent
versions.subtitle: All Mechanisms, One Loop
versions.keyInsight: The final harness is still one loop, now surrounded by the systems that make it production-shaped.

functions.name: Assemble
functions.signature: static string Assemble(Dictionary<string, string> sections, Dictionary<string, object> ctx)
